title: Turkish-Style Lamb Pide
description: A delicious and authentic Turkish dish featuring tender lamb and crispy pide bread, served with a side of fresh herbs and spices.
instructions: Roll out the pide dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Cut the lamb shoulder into 1-inch cubes and season with salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the lamb to a baking sheet and set aside.
Place the pide dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the dough with olive oil and sprinkle with minced garlic.
Bake the pide in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the pide is baking, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the lamb cubes back into the skillet and cook until heated through.
Serve the lamb with the crispy pide bread and garnish with fresh parsley.
